items: What has been going well for you this week?
What has been difficult for you this past week?
How have you experienced God this week?
How can we help you with your needs?
What would you like prayer for?
What have you put into practice since our last meeting?
Were you able to share a God experience with anyone? Did you pray with them?
Have one person retell the passage in their own words with the group’s help.
Was there anything missed or added?

data.text: <sup>3</sup>The teachers of the law and the Pharisees brought in a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before the group<sup>4</sup>and said to Jesus, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.<sup> 5 </sup>In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?”<sup>6</sup>They were using this question as a trap, in order to have a basis for accusing him.

But Jesus bent down and started to write on the ground with his finger.<sup> 7 </sup>When they kept on questioning him, he straightened up and said to them, “Let any one of you who is without sin be the first to throw a stone at her.”<sup>8</sup>Again he stooped down and wrote on the ground.

<sup>9</sup>At this, those who heard began to go away one at a time, the older ones first, until only Jesus was left, with the woman still standing there.<sup> 10 </sup>Jesus straightened up and asked her, “Woman, where are they? Has no one condemned you?”
“Then neither do I condemn you,” Jesus declared. “Go now and leave your life of sin.”

items: What stands out for you in this passage?
What do you like and what bothers you in this passage?
What does this passage tell you about Jesus and people?
What does this passage say about judgment?
What are some possible ways a person could apply this passage? Is there: A behaviour to change?A promise to claim?An example to follow?A command to obey?
Ask God what He wants you to do in response to the passage.
